current_judgment: Baseline (qwen2.5:14b) and challenger (qwen3:14b) runs complete. Both models returned schema_validation_status: valid on all 5 scenes. Schema reliability 1.00 for both. The voice_consistency miscategorization on scene-04 is confirmed as systematic across both models: neither lowers voice_consistency meaningfully for an explicit POV shift — qwen2.5:14b scored 0.85, qwen3:14b scored 0.90. Both models locate the first-person intrusion in evidence spans but attribute it to flow/pacing at the dimension score level. This is a prompt-level calibration gap, not a model capability gap. qwen3:14b produces richer finding labels (explicit 'Perspective shifts' observation) and 3 evidence spans per scene vs 2, but shows systematic score inflation across most dimensions, reducing discriminative power on low-scoring scenes (sentence_variety on scene-03-flat: 0.60 vs qwen2.5's 0.30; voice_consistency on scene-03-flat: 0.90 vs 0.50). qwen2.5:14b remains the preferred model for its more conservative and diagnostically useful scoring profile. qwen3:14b is not the preferred model candidate. The next action is prompt clarification of the voice_consistency definition to include POV fidelity.
next_required_decision: The voice_consistency miscategorization is confirmed systematic across both qwen2.5:14b (baseline) and qwen3:14b (challenger). The gap is at the prompt level: voice_consistency is not defined to include POV fidelity. Update the style-analysis-scene-v1 prompt to explicitly state that voice_consistency penalizes POV-perspective drift. Re-test both models on scene-04 and at least 2 additional POV-shift scenes against the updated prompt. If the updated prompt resolves the miscategorization, assess readiness for candidate_baseline promotion with qwen2.5:14b as the baseline model. qwen3:14b should not be adopted as the baseline until score inflation is addressed or evaluated as acceptable for the use case.
provenance_notes: Metrics derived from operator judgment across 5 eval scenes (run-2026-03-21-001 through run-2026-03-21-005). schema_reliability: 5/5 runs returned valid schema envelope. false_positive_rate: 0 clearly incorrect findings identified across all 5 runs. surface_detection_rate: 4/4 targeted style issues detected (scene-04 counted as detected despite dimension miscategorization). All metrics are operator-judged from a small 5-scene set. Not benchmark-derived. Not gate-eligible. metric_profile is set to detection_reasoning as the closest available value; style analysis identifies issues and reasons about them but is not a binary detection task.
